history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed during the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade because the Spring Security DSL method authorizeRequests() was not migrated to authorizeHttpRequests(), leaving requestMatchers() calls invalid under Spring Security 6.
history.qwen_rationale: The base UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 meta-recipe sometimes misses non-standard security configurations or leaves authorizeRequests() intact. Adding SpringBoot3BestPractices to the same step triggers additional Spring Security 6 migration recipes and handles @MockBean -> @MockitoBean replacements, ensuring the code compiles cleanly under the new framework versions before proceeding to the JDK 21 build upgrade.

history.qwen_observation: The build failed during the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade step because the git-commit-id-plugin threw a TransportException (Missing delta base) while fetching git data, causing mvn compile to abort.
history.qwen_rationale: Injecting a step to set git.skip=true in the POM will bypass the git-commit-id-plugin execution during compilation, preventing the git fetch error and allowing the Spring Boot and JDK 21 migrations to proceed.

history.qwen_observation: The upgrade_spring_boot_3_3 step failed during recipe application due to a Git transport exception caused by the git-commit-id-plugin attempting to fetch in an isolated environment, despite a prior git.skip=true property addition.
history.qwen_rationale: Forcing multiple git-commit-id-plugin skip properties (git.skip, git.commit.id.skip, git.failOnNoGitDirectory) via AddProperty ensures the plugin is completely bypassed during the Maven lifecycle triggered by rewrite:run. The rest of the chain follows the standard Spring Boot 2.7 -> 3.3 and Java 17 -> 21 migration path.

history.qwen_observation: The git-commit-id-plugin fails during mvn compile with a JGit TransportException (Missing delta base), likely due to a shallow/corrupted clone in the CI environment, and previous AddProperty attempts to skip it were insufficient.
history.qwen_rationale: Reinforcing skip properties (git.skip, git.commit.id.skip) and explicitly setting git.failOnNoGitDirectory=false should prevent the plugin from attempting network fetches or failing on missing directories. The Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade step remains to handle the javax->jakarta and Spring Security 6 API migrations required for Java 21 compatibility.

history.qwen_observation: The git-commit-id-plugin fails during the build phase with TransportException: Missing delta base due to a shallow or corrupted .git state, blocking the Spring Boot 3 upgrade step.
history.qwen_rationale: Reinforcing git skip properties and upgrading the plugin to its final stable version (4.9.10) should prevent JGit fetch attempts that crash the build. The chain then proceeds with plugin upgrades, Spring Boot 3.3 migration (which handles Security 6 API changes), and Java 21 build/transforms.
